did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper proposes an algorithmic method called 'ID-BISIM' (inverse SNR-decorrelation with Binary Image SIMilarity) for automatic 3D adaptive vessel segmentation in OCTA images of Port Wine Stain (PWS) skin. The method relies on a linear relationship between intensity and complex-decorrelation and uses a binary image similarity index to determine an optimal threshold. It does not employ machine learning, deep learning, or artificial intelligence techniques; rather, it uses a deterministic, rule-based computational approach for image processing and segmentation.

non_compliance_reason: The paper does not use AI to obtain results in dermatology.
